Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Your Impact as a Founding Partner & Lead Veterinarian

This is more than a leadership role; it's an opportunity to build and lead a practice with the full backing of an established network. As the Founding Partner & Lead Veterinarian, you will be the cornerstone of the hospital, responsible for establishing a gold-standard medical culture, mentoring your team, and driving the business's success as a true owner.

Build a Thriving Practice Culture: You will be the architect of a client-first, collaborative culture, setting the tone for medical excellence and teamwork from day one.
Lead and Mentor Your Medical Team: You will recruit, onboard, and develop your associate veterinarians and paraprofessional staff, fostering a team that is highly skilled, efficient, and engaged.
Drive Medical and Surgical Excellence: You will set and maintain the hospital's standard of care, ensuring the delivery of excellent patient care and providing mentorship in general surgery and dentistry.
Partner in Business Ownership:You will work directly with your Hospital Manager to manage the hospital's financial performance, making strategic decisions that drive top-line revenue growth and long-term success.

What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a license in good standing to practice.
A minimum of 4+ years of experience as a veterinarian, with a strong foundation in general medicine, surgery, and dentistry.
A compassionate and collaborative leadership style with the ability to mentor and develop associate veterinarians and support staff.
An entrepreneurial spirit and a strong understanding of what it takes to run a successful practice.
Previous formal leadership or management experience is a significant plus.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Livewell Animal Hospital San Diego offers you the chance to own a premium practice in Downtown San Diego’s Marina District. One of the most vibrant and high-traffic neighborhoods. Located along a key downtown corridor with seamless access between the Convention Center and I-5, your hospital will be positioned for maximum visibility and accessibility. Within just a quarter mile, over 1,500 residential units create a built-in client base, while proximity to RaDD and Campus at Horton places you in the heart of the city’s most exciting growth and innovation hubs.

Livewell Animal Hospitals are built, sparing no expense, with the mission of offering a boutique or concierge-level client experience. Features include 5 exam rooms, dedicated surgery suites, a dedicated dental suite, in-house labs, Easy Vet PIMS, to name a few! The environment is tailored for both top-tier patient care and a premium client experience. 

This location offers not only a thriving business opportunity but a lifestyle surrounded by bayfront views, walkable urban amenities, and a dynamic downtown culture. If you're ready to take ownership in a hospital that blends luxury, location, and leading-edge care—this is it.

Your Impact as a Founding Partner & Lead Veterinarian

As the Founding Partner & Lead Veterinarian, you will be the cornerstone of the hospital, responsible for establishing a gold-standard medical culture, mentoring your team, and driving the business's success as a true owner.

Build a Thriving Practice Culture: You will be the architect of a client-first, collaborative culture, setting the tone for medical excellence and teamwork from day one.

Partner in Business Ownership:You will work directly with your Hospital Manager to manage the hospital's financial performance, making strategic decisions that drive top-line revenue growth and long-term success.

What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a license in good standing to practice.
A minimum of 4+ years of experience as a veterinarian, with a strong foundation in general medicine, surgery, and dentistry.
A compassionate and collaborative leadership style with the ability to mentor and develop associate veterinarians and support staff.

Previous formal leadership or management experience is a significant plus.

Job Title: Urgent Care Veterinarian in Green Bay WI
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description

Additional Hospital Information & Who We are Looking For: 

Looking for a flexible schedule and caseload? 

• Interest In: Small-animal general practice in an AAHA-accredited hospital committed to high-quality, progressive medicine and an excellent client experience. Clinical interests may include preventative care, comprehensive dentistry, surgery and advanced surgical procedures, senior pet care, dermatology, and the option to participate in urgent care and same-day sick visits. The practice also offers exposure to pocket pets and avian medicine, providing variety for veterinarians interested in a diverse caseload.

 

• GP, Urgent Care, or Hybrid Opportunities: This hospital offers exceptional flexibility with openings for General Practice, Urgent Care, or a Hybrid GP/Urgent Care role. Schedules and caseloads can be tailored based on your interests, experience, and career goals, making this an ideal opportunity for veterinarians seeking variety, pace, and balance.

 

• Specialized & Expanded Services: In addition to routine and advanced medicine, the hospital supports orthopedic procedures, abdominal surgery, advanced nose and eye capabilities, and behavioral medicine. The practice also features doggy daycare, a bone-shaped pool, and additional training services, creating a truly comprehensive and engaging environment for both patients and clients.

 

• Supportive, Experienced Technical Team: Practice alongside a highly skilled CVT team and long-tenured support staff with strong capabilities in anesthesia monitoring, dentistry, diagnostics, patient handling, and client education. The team is known for being happy, collaborative, and low-turnover, creating a genuinely supportive work environment.

 

• Layout & Facility Highlights: AAHA-accredited, modern facility featuring multiple exam rooms, a dedicated surgery suite, a full dental suite, ABAXIS in-house laboratory, ultrasound imaging, digital radiography, electrocautery, blood transfusion capabilities, echocardiography, and other advanced equipment — all designed to support efficient workflows and high-quality medicine every day.

 

• Collaborative, Growing Doctor Team: Join a 4-DVM team with room to grow, operating under new leadership with a renewed focus on teamwork, communication, and clinic culture. This is a fast-paced, engaging environment where no two days are the same, but where support and collaboration remain central.

 

• Patient Mix: A diverse small-animal caseload including dogs, cats, pocket pets, and birds, offering both routine and complex cases for veterinarians who enjoy variety and continued learning.

 

• Flexible Scheduling: Schedules are designed with flexibility in mind, supporting work-life balance while maintaining a dynamic, fulfilling clinical experience.

 

Website: https://www.animalhousegreenbay.com/
Location: 3171 Voyager Dr, Green Bay, WI 54311

 

Apply today for immediate consideration!

 If you have the drive, passion, and medical skills we’re looking for, we’d love to meet you.

For confidential consideration, please send your resume to Katie Shaffer at: katie.shaffer@mvetpartners.com

 

All applications and conversations are upheld with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

 

Why Green Bay, WI?

Located along the shores of Lake Michigan, Green Bay offers a welcoming community feel with a strong mix of outdoor recreation, affordability, and Midwest charm. Residents enjoy waterfront trails, parks, and nearby nature preserves, along with a growing food scene, local breweries, arts and cultural events, and year-round festivals.

With a lower cost of living, easy commutes, and family-friendly neighborhoods, Green Bay is an ideal place to settle long-term. Its close proximity to Door County makes weekend getaways easy, while abundant year-round recreational opportunities support a balanced lifestyle that blends professional fulfillment with a high quality of life.

 

What Your Day-to-Day Looks Like
Build Lasting Relationships: Connect with clients and their pets, becoming their trusted partner in a pet's lifelong health.
Practice Your Best Medicine: From diagnosis to treatment, you'll lead patient care with the freedom to uphold the highest standards.
Educate and Empower: Clearly communicate findings and treatment plans, empowering clients to make informed decisions.
Collaborate and Grow: Work alongside a supportive paraprofessional team, contributing to a positive hospital culture.
Who We're Looking For
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree.
A valid state license and in good standing to practice.
A collaborative spirit and a dedication to practicing the highest standard of medicine.
Excellent communication skills with a positive, friendly attitude.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Job Title: Medical Lead Veterinarian in Wauwatosa WI at Mayfair Animal Hospital & ER
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description

Additional Hospital Information & Who We are Looking For: 

Mayfair Animal Hospital & ER is seeking a Medical Lead and is open to experienced ER veterinarians as well as board-certified or residency-trained specialists (Critical Care, Internal Medicine, Surgery) interested in stepping into a leadership role. Our hospital is well-equipped and designed for advanced medicine, allowing you to step in and make an immediate impact.

• Interest In: Small-animal general practice medicine including wellness exams, preventative care, diagnostics, dentistry, soft tissue surgery, senior care, pain management, and client education with leadership involvement in urgent and emergency care workflows. This role is ideal for a veterinarian who enjoys both practicing high-quality medicine and helping guide clinical standards, case management, and team development in a dynamic GP + urgent care environment.

• Medical Lead / Leadership Role: We are seeking an experienced veterinarian interested in stepping into a Medical Lead (Lead DVM) role. This position blends hands-on clinical practice with leadership responsibilities, including mentoring associate veterinarians, collaborating with hospital leadership, supporting medical protocols, and helping foster a positive, collaborative culture. Prior leadership experience is a plus but not required for the right candidate with strong communication skills and a desire to lead by example.

Medical Lead Role Snapshot

This is a primarily clinical role, with focused leadership responsibilities centered on medical quality, standards, and direction for the hospital.

Clinical Responsibilities (80–90%)

Communicate clearly and compassionately with clients regarding diagnostics, treatment options, and outcomes
Collaborate closely with technicians and support staff to ensure efficient, high-quality patient care
Lead by example on the floor, modeling excellent medicine, professionalism, and client communication

Medical Lead / Leader of Medicine Responsibilities (10–20%)

Serve as the medical leader for the hospital, helping guide clinical standards, protocols, and quality-of-care initiatives
Support consistency in medical decision-making and case management across the doctor team
Act as a clinical sounding board for complex cases, protocol questions, and medical direction
Promote a culture of medical excellence, accountability, and continuous improvement

• Supportive, Experienced Technical & Clinical Team: Work alongside a highly skilled technician and support team experienced in both general practice and urgent/emergency workflows. The Medical Lead will partner closely with staff to promote efficient case flow, strong medical standards, and excellent patient and client experiences.

• Layout Specifics: AAHA-accredited, full-service hospital featuring multiple exam rooms, treatment areas, surgical and dental suites, in-house laboratory, digital radiology, in-house pharmacy, and infrastructure designed to support both scheduled appointments and urgent/emergency cases — allowing the Medical Lead to practice and guide full-scope medicine.

• Collaborative Leadership Environment: The Medical Lead will work in close partnership with hospital management and regional clinical leadership to support medical quality, mentorship, and team engagement. This role emphasizes approachable leadership, shared decision-making, and creating an environment where doctors and staff feel supported and empowered.

• Patient Mix: A diverse and engaging caseload including wellness and preventative care, same-day urgent visits, and emergency cases. The Medical Lead will help guide medical decision-making while remaining clinically active, maintaining credibility and connection with the day-to-day flow of the hospital.

Website: https://mayfairanimalemergency.com/
Location: 11637 W. North Ave, Wauwatosa, WI 53226

Job Title: Associate Veterinarian in Green Bay WI
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description

Additional Hospital Information & Who We are Looking For:

Looking for a flexible schedule and caseload? 

• Interest In: Small-animal general practice in an AAHA-accredited hospital committed to high-quality, progressive medicine and an excellent client experience. Clinical interests may include preventative care, comprehensive dentistry, surgery and advanced surgical procedures, senior pet care, dermatology, and the option to participate in urgent care and same-day sick visits. The practice also offers exposure to pocket pets and avian medicine, providing variety for veterinarians interested in a diverse caseload.

 

• GP, Urgent Care, or Hybrid Opportunities: This hospital offers exceptional flexibility with openings for General Practice, Urgent Care, or a Hybrid GP/Urgent Care role. Schedules and caseloads can be tailored based on your interests, experience, and career goals, making this an ideal opportunity for veterinarians seeking variety, pace, and balance.

 

• Specialized & Expanded Services: In addition to routine and advanced medicine, the hospital supports orthopedic procedures, abdominal surgery, advanced nose and eye capabilities, and behavioral medicine. The practice also features doggy daycare, a bone-shaped pool, and additional training services, creating a truly comprehensive and engaging environment for both patients and clients.

 

• Open to New Graduates or Experienced Veterinarians: We welcome both new graduates and experienced veterinarians, with tailored mentorship available to support doctors at every stage of their career. New grads will find a supportive, encouraging learning environment, while experienced doctors will enjoy autonomy, collaboration, and the opportunity to further develop clinical interests.

 

• Supportive, Experienced Technical Team: Practice alongside a highly skilled CVT team and long-tenured support staff with strong capabilities in anesthesia monitoring, dentistry, diagnostics, patient handling, and client education. The team is known for being happy, collaborative, and low-turnover, creating a genuinely supportive work environment.

 

• Layout & Facility Highlights: AAHA-accredited, modern facility featuring multiple exam rooms, a dedicated surgery suite, a full dental suite, ABAXIS in-house laboratory, ultrasound imaging, digital radiography, electrocautery, blood transfusion capabilities, echocardiography, and other advanced equipment — all designed to support efficient workflows and high-quality medicine every day.

 

• Collaborative, Growing Doctor Team: Join a 4-DVM team with room to grow, operating under new leadership with a renewed focus on teamwork, communication, and clinic culture. This is a fast-paced, engaging environment where no two days are the same, but where support and collaboration remain central.

 

• Patient Mix: A diverse small-animal caseload including dogs, cats, pocket pets, and birds, offering both routine and complex cases for veterinarians who enjoy variety and continued learning.

 

• Flexible Scheduling: Schedules are designed with flexibility in mind, supporting work-life balance while maintaining a dynamic, fulfilling clinical experience.

 

Website: https://www.animalhousegreenbay.com/
Location: 3171 Voyager Dr, Green Bay, WI 54311

For confidential consideration, please send your resume to Katie Shaffer at: katie.shaffer@mvetpartners.com

 

All applications and conversations are upheld with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

 

Why Green Bay, WI?

Located along the shores of Lake Michigan, Green Bay offers a welcoming community feel with a strong mix of outdoor recreation, affordability, and Midwest charm. Residents enjoy waterfront trails, parks, and nearby nature preserves, along with a growing food scene, local breweries, arts and cultural events, and year-round festivals.

With a lower cost of living, easy commutes, and family-friendly neighborhoods, Green Bay is an ideal place to settle long-term. Its close proximity to Door County makes weekend getaways easy, while abundant year-round recreational opportunities support a balanced lifestyle that blends professional fulfillment with a high quality of life.

Job Title: Lead Veterinarian Opportunity at Willamette Veterinary Hospital
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Mission Pet Health is seeking a Regional Medical Director (RMD) to support our hospitals in Seattle, Boise, Salt Lake and Spokane.
This role is designed for a veterinarian who enjoys mentoring doctors, strengthening hospital teams, and helping practices grow while maintaining high medical quality.
As Regional Medical Director, you will serve as a clinical partner to our veterinarians and hospitals, supporting teams at the local level while working alongside a Regional Operations Director to align medical leadership with hospital success. The role blends mentorship, collaboration, and strategic leadership—ensuring doctors feel supported, hospitals are well-resourced, and patients receive excellent care.
What You'll Do
Support and Develop Veterinarians: Partner with DVMs across the region to support their professional growth through mentorship, coaching, and development planning. Conduct doctor reviews and help strengthen medical, surgical, and communication skills.
Strengthen Hospital Collaboration: Connect regularly with hospital teams to share best practices, support workflow improvements, and ensure doctors have the clinical resources they need to serve their communities.
Recruit and Develop Veterinary Talent: Help attract and onboard great veterinarians to the region. Support mentorship and integration of new doctors to ensure long-term success and engagement.
Partner for Hospital Success: Work closely with your Regional Operations Director to support hospital leaders in areas such as scheduling needs, equipment investments, and aligning medical initiatives with hospital growth.
Support Growth in the PacWest Market: Help hospitals expand services, strengthen medical capabilities, and support thoughtful regional growth while maintaining high-quality medicine.
Build the Future Veterinary Workforce: Support externship and internship programs and represent Mission Pet Health within the veterinary community to help build our future talent pipeline.
What Success Looks Like in the First Year
First 90 Days: Build relationships with veterinarians and hospital teams while learning the strengths and opportunities of each hospital in the region.
3–6 Months: Establish regular doctor collaboration and mentorship, support hospital teams with clinical resources, and assist with recruiting and onboarding veterinarians.
6–12 Months: Strengthen collaboration across hospitals, support service expansion where appropriate, and help develop emerging medical leaders within the region.
Why This Role is Different
At Mission Pet Health, our Regional Medical Directors focus on supporting hospitals—not overseeing them from a distance.
We believe great veterinary medicine happens when doctors feel trusted, connected, and supported. Our RMDs focus on:
Mentorship over management
Local hospital partnership

Collaboration across veterinary teams
Balancing medical quality with sustainable hospital growth
This is an opportunity to help shape the veterinary culture of a region—supporting great hospitals and helping veterinarians build long-term, fulfilling careers.

What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree from an accredited university.
Current state licensure in good standing to practice.
Experience managing multi-site veterinary hospitals is required.
Hospital ownership or previous Lead Veterinarian experience is strongly preferred.
Exceptional interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills with the ability to present key data concisely.
Strong organizational skills and a keen attention to detail.

Job Description
Your Impact as an Acquisition Diligence Analyst

Mission is seeking a highly qualified Financial Diligence Analyst to join our rapidly growing team in Birmingham, AL. This role will be an integral part of Mission's finance team, and will be an essential partner to our business development team. The primary responsibilities of our financial diligence team center around confirmation of the quality of earnings ("QoE") of target hospitals, multi-year forecasting of target hospitals and valuation modeling for all acquisitions and capex projects. This position has reporting responsibilities to executives, business development, finance, accounting and operations.

Your Impact Will Include:
Assisting in the preparation of our internal QoE workbook, which is used to analyze hospital results and identify risks, opportunities and adjustments.
Collaborating with business development, hospital seller(s), and sell-side advisors to collect, analyze, and understand transaction level detail in the hospital’s historical financial results.
Preparing pro forma financial results and forecasting the next 24 months for both target acquisitions with input from our operations teams and business intelligence team.
Preparing a pro forma forecast and calculating the five-year return for hospital capex projects with input from our operations team.
Assisting in the creation of investment committee memorandums for both acquisitions and capex projects.
Conducting detailed financial analyses and providing insights to supervisors regarding hospital performance.
Assisting in the preparation of quarterly board of director slides.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in financial analysis, public accounting (TAS, audit) or a similar role.
Strong proficiency in financial modeling and analysis tools (e.g., Excel, financial software).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
Commitment to our WAG values.

Job Description

The Human Resource Business Partner contributes to the company performance by providing tactical and strategic partnership on people and organization development strategies in support of the company, regional and hospital business objectives. The Business Partner carries out responsibilities in the following functional areas: teammate relations, policy interpretation and application, performance, compensation and talent management.

Partners with Divisional and Regional leadership to align HR strategy to business strategy.
Studies trends and HR metrics to develop solutions, programs and policies that drive engagement, retention, and operational efficiency.
Provides day-to-day coaching to leadership on a variety of actions to include employee relations issues, policy interpretation and application and talent management and development.
Evaluates organizational issues, patterns and trends to proactively recommend and design HR  solutions, tools and training for leaders.
Leads and conducts internal investigations related to allegations of discrimination, harassment, hostile work environment, or other teammate relations matters ensuring fair and thorough resolution.
Partners with Legal to manage EEOC claims.
Supports and champions company-wide HR initiatives such as Open Enrollment, Performance Evaluations, Merit Cycle, Engagement Survey, etc.
Collaborates with Talent Acquisition and Total Rewards teams to ensure compensation structures and hiring practices align with market trends and internal equity.
Provides HR data analysis and reporting to business leaders, highlighting trends and actionable insights related to turnover, engagement, and performance.
Serves as a trusted advisor to leadership, promoting ethical practices and decision-making that reflect the organization's mission, vision, and values
Up to 25% travel required.

Requirements

Bachelor’s degree in a related field required
5+ years of HR experience
Professional Certification preferred

Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.

Job Description
Mission Pet Health is seeking a Regional Medical Director (RMD) to support our hospitals in the Austin area.
This role is designed for a veterinarian who enjoys mentoring doctors, strengthening hospital teams, and helping practices grow while maintaining high medical quality.
As Regional Medical Director, you will serve as a clinical partner to our veterinarians and hospitals, supporting teams at the local level while working alongside a Regional Operations Director to align medical leadership with hospital success. The role blends mentorship, collaboration, and strategic leadership—ensuring doctors feel supported, hospitals are well-resourced, and patients receive excellent care.
What You'll Do
Support and Develop Veterinarians: Partner with DVMs across the region to support their professional growth through mentorship, coaching, and development planning. Conduct doctor reviews and help strengthen medical, surgical, and communication skills.
Strengthen Hospital Collaboration: Connect regularly with hospital teams to share best practices, support workflow improvements, and ensure doctors have the clinical resources they need to serve their communities.
Recruit and Develop Veterinary Talent: Help attract and onboard great veterinarians to the region. Support mentorship and integration of new doctors to ensure long-term success and engagement.
Partner for Hospital Success: Work closely with your Regional Operations Director to support hospital leaders in areas such as scheduling needs, equipment investments, and aligning medical initiatives with hospital growth.
Support Growth in the Houston Market: Help hospitals expand services, strengthen medical capabilities, and support thoughtful regional growth while maintaining high-quality medicine.
Build the Future Veterinary Workforce: Support externship and internship programs and represent Mission Pet Health within the veterinary community to help build our future talent pipeline.
What Success Looks Like in the First Year
First 90 Days: Build relationships with veterinarians and hospital teams while learning the strengths and opportunities of each hospital in the region.
3–6 Months: Establish regular doctor collaboration and mentorship, support hospital teams with clinical resources, and assist with recruiting and onboarding veterinarians.
6–12 Months: Strengthen collaboration across hospitals, support service expansion where appropriate, and help develop emerging medical leaders within the region.
Why This Role is Different
At Mission Pet Health, our Regional Medical Directors focus on supporting hospitals—not overseeing them from a distance.
We believe great veterinary medicine happens when doctors feel trusted, connected, and supported. Our RMDs focus on:
Mentorship over management
Local hospital partnership

Collaboration across veterinary teams
Balancing medical quality with sustainable hospital growth
This is an opportunity to help shape the veterinary culture of a region—supporting great hospitals and helping veterinarians build long-term, fulfilling careers.

What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree from an accredited university.
Current state licensure in good standing to practice.
Experience managing multi-site veterinary hospitals is required.
Hospital ownership or previous Lead Veterinarian experience is strongly preferred.
Exceptional interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills with the ability to present key data concisely.
Strong organizational skills and a keen attention to detail.

Job Description

Veterinary Urgent Care Center's (VUCC) mission is to change veterinary medicine for the better, both for our patients and pet owners, and for the talented women and men who make this profession their life's work. Since 2017, VUCC has been a leader in the veterinary urgent care space in Massachusetts and is expanding our services into new communities in the Northeast.

 

VUCC is currently searching for a GP Veterinarian with a focus on dental cases to work at our Worcester location. Urgent care shifts available.

 

Full time Veterinarians work a standard 28 scheduled clinical hours in approximately 4, 7-hour shifts. Join the team and be a part of the growing field of veterinary urgent care medicine!

Benefits

VUCC boasts a robust benefits package in partnership with Mission Pet Health, a veterinary owned and operated network of animal hospitals throughout the country.

 

Full-time benefits include:

Competitive salary range $160,000 - $240,000+ annually depending on PT vs FT and experience
level
 Medical, Dental, Vision, 401k match, among other benefits.
Full time positions are standard 28 scheduled clinical hours. Schedules can be flexible or set and prioritized for a healthy work/life balance. 50% weekend coverage is standard but can be rotating or set.
Full Time Veterinarians are provided a medical transcriptionist
Non-operational days annually: New years day, Memorial day, 4th of July Labor Day, Thanksgiving day, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, New Years Day
Paid time off with no negative accrual
Generous CE compensation

MISSION | VISION | VALUES

Veterinary Urgent Care Center's mission is to change veterinary medicine for the better, both for our patients and pet owners, and for the talented women and men who make this profession their life's work.

 

We firmly believe that changes need to happen in the veterinary field to keep and attract the best talent into our industry. We respect you, the person, over anything else, and have created a culture that supports our teammates through the good and bad days we all experience in veterinary medicine. We deliver on our mission and build towards our vision with a set of values that we strive to live by every day. These values are core to how we work, act, and interact with each other.

 

Authenticity matters…what you see is what you get with us! We treat others the way they want to be treated and understand that not every day is going to be someone's best. We support, listen, extend a hand and keep it real with our teammates. 

 

Respect is earned and expected…we may not always agree, but we do so with a deep respect for one another. Respect is expected from everyone within our practice. We have a zero tolerance policy for bullying, name calling, negative treatment of others, or anything that doesn't fit into the criteria of our core values.

 

Compassion goes a long way….everybody's situation is different and we show people grace for whatever is happening in their lives. If you are going through situations in your life, our team will work with you, without judgment, to grant you the space or support to get through life's challenges.

 

Balance is more important than ever… we know that there is more to life than just work. We understand the challenges that come from veterinary medicine, and we have developed a model that gives you the support and time off you need to prevent burnout.

 

You have a voice here…we welcome your thoughts and ideas and are always willing to listen and learn. Communication is key in all of our interactions, so speak up!

 

VUCC provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment in all job classifications without regard to race, color, religion, age, mental disability, physical disability, medical condition, gender, sexual orientation, genetic information, ancestry, marital status, national origin, veteran status, and other classifications protected by applicable state and local non-discrimination laws.

Job Description
Your Impact as a Founding Partner & Lead Veterinarian

This is more than a leadership role; it's an opportunity to build and lead a practice with the full backing of an established network. As the Founding Partner & Lead Veterinarian, you will be the cornerstone of the hospital, responsible for establishing a gold-standard medical culture, mentoring your team, and driving the business's success as a true owner.

Build a Thriving Practice Culture: You will be the architect of a client-first, collaborative culture, setting the tone for medical excellence and teamwork from day one.
Lead and Mentor Your Medical Team: You will recruit, onboard, and develop your associate veterinarians and paraprofessional staff, fostering a team that is highly skilled, efficient, and engaged.
Drive Medical and Surgical Excellence: You will set and maintain the hospital's standard of care, ensuring the delivery of excellent patient care and providing mentorship in general surgery and dentistry.
Partner in Business Ownership: You will work directly with your Hospital Manager to manage the hospital's financial performance, making strategic decisions that drive top-line revenue growth and long-term success.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a license in good standing to practice.
A minimum of 4+ years of experience as a veterinarian, with a strong foundation in general medicine, surgery, and dentistry.
A compassionate and collaborative leadership style with the ability to mentor and develop associate veterinarians and support staff.
An entrepreneurial spirit and a strong understanding of what it takes to run a successful practice.
Previous formal leadership or management experience is a significant plus.

Recruit and Develop Veterinary Talent: Help attract and onboard great veterinarians to the region. Support mentorship and integration of new doctors to ensure long-term success and engagement.
Partner for Hospital Success: Work closely with your Regional Operations Director to support hospital leaders in areas such as scheduling needs, equipment investments, and aligning medical initiatives with hospital growth.
Support Growth in the Houston Market: Help hospitals expand services, strengthen medical capabilities, and support thoughtful regional growth while maintaining high-quality medicine.
Build the Future Veterinary Workforce: Support externship and internship programs and represent Mission Pet Health within the veterinary community to help build our future talent pipeline.
What Success Looks Like in the First Year
First 90 Days: Build relationships with veterinarians and hospital teams while learning the strengths and opportunities of each hospital in the region.
3–6 Months: Establish regular doctor collaboration and mentorship, support hospital teams with clinical resources, and assist with recruiting and onboarding veterinarians.
6–12 Months: Strengthen collaboration across hospitals, support service expansion where appropriate, and help develop emerging medical leaders within the region.
Why This Role is Different
At Mission Pet Health, our Regional Medical Directors focus on supporting hospitals—not overseeing them from a distance.
We believe great veterinary medicine happens when doctors feel trusted, connected, and supported. Our RMDs focus on:
Mentorship over management
Local hospital partnership

Collaboration across veterinary teams
Balancing medical quality with sustainable hospital growth
This is an opportunity to help shape the veterinary culture of a region—supporting great hospitals and helping veterinarians build long-term, fulfilling careers.

What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree from an accredited university.
Current state licensure in good standing to practice.
Experience managing multi-site veterinary hospitals is required.
Hospital ownership or previous Lead Veterinarian experience is strongly preferred.
Exceptional interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills with the ability to present key data concisely.
Strong organizational skills and a keen attention to detail.

Job Title: Medical Lead Veterinarian at Glendale Animal Hospital
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Job Title: Medical Lead - Boston Veterinary Clinic, Brookline
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Job Description

Strong Village Veterinary Center in Livonia, MI is growing! We are looking for an Board Certified Full-Time Veterinary Dental Specialist to join our wonderful team. 

About our Role: 

Strong Village Veterinary Center is a full-service, 6-doctor small animal hospital located in Livonia, Michigan. We have been serving our community for over 40 years! This is an opportunity to take over a long-term, successful dentistry service, working in a practice with a central location within booming community and outstanding reputation. We are open to speaking to current residents,practice-limited candidates that are sitting for boards within the next year, and to board certified Dental Specialists. It is our commitment to provide quality, veterinary care throughout our patients' lives.

 Our services and facilities are designed to assist in routine preventive care for young, healthy pets; early detection and treatment of disease as pets age; and complete medical, surgical, and urgent care as necessary during their lifetime. In addition to offering advanced dental care, our clinic offers a variety of services including acupuncture, laser therapy and ultrasound. Our facility has Heska in house lab, digital x-rays, cutting edge laser and therapeutic cold laser and new ultrasound equipment! 

Our medical leadership teams are committed to supporting career development, continuing education, and reaching personal goals. All Veterinarians will find interesting, diverse, and challenging cases, with a manageable caseload. Our clinic promotes a respectful, educational, collaborative, and fun workplace environment. 

About Livonia, MI Located in the western suburbs of Metro Detroit - Livonia is centered halfway between Downtown Detroit and Ann Arbor with great hotels, fantastic shopping and dining options. It is a short drive to the many museums, professional sporting events, theaters and culture of a revitalized downtown Detroit. Also, near by you can explore The Henry Ford, which includes Greenfield Village and the Ford Rouge Factory Tour. Livonia also boasts extraordinary parks and recreation! Livonia is a place to live, learn, work and play that is within a convenient drive to most of metro Detroits destinations. Livonia has big-city convenience and small-town closeness that provide a distinctive community spirit. 

Requirements: - Licensed Michigan Veterinarian (active and clear) - Board Certified Veterinary Dentist Our 

Offer: - Competitive Guaranteed Base Salary - Production Bonus with NO negative accrual! - Signing Bonus - Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance - Paid Maternity / Paternity Leave - Company Paid Wellness Programs - Paid license fees - 401(K) with Employer Match - Paid Time Off - Paid Continuing Education + dedicated days off to attend 

If this sounds like a great career option for you, APPLY Today for Immediate Consideration!

Job Title: Associate Veterinarian in New Berlin WI
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description

Additional Hospital Information & What We are Looking For: 

• Interest In: Small-animal general practice, including wellness exams, preventative care, diagnostics, dental services, soft tissue surgery, laboratory services, senior wellness, vaccinations, microchipping, pain management, spay/neuter, and compassionate client education. The practice also offers avian and exotic pet care, providing variety for doctors who want exposure to those species while maintaining a strong primary small-animal GP focus.

 

• Open to New Graduates or Experienced Veterinarians: New grads looking to build their skills in a supportive, hands-on environment are welcomed alongside experienced veterinarians eager to contribute meaningful medicine and mentorship. The team values continuous learning and collaborative growth.

 

• Supportive, Experienced Technical Staff: A skilled and collaborative support team that understands urgent care workflows and works closely with doctors to support diagnostics, patient handling, anesthesia support, client communication, and efficient case flow allowing veterinarians to focus on medicine even during high-acuity periods.

 

• Layout Specifics: Full-service facility with multiple exam rooms, treatment areas, surgical and dental suites, in-house laboratory services, digital radiology, pharmacy access, and structured workflows designed to support comprehensive small-animal care with comfort and efficiency.

 

• Collaborative Team Environment: A team-oriented culture that treats pets like family and prioritizes compassionate, personalized care. Collaboration across doctors and support staff ensures thoughtful case planning, client education, and continuity of care.

 

• Patient Mix: Primarily dogs and cats across all life stages, with opportunities to care for avian and exotic patients for doctors with interest in those areas, providing a well-rounded and engaging general practice caseload.

 

Website: https://newberlinvet.com/
 Location: 13100 W National Ave, New Berlin, WI 53151

Apply today for immediate consideration!
If you have the drive, passion, and medical skills we’re looking for, we’d love to meet you.

 For confidential consideration, please send your resume to Katie Shaffer at: katie.shaffer@mvetpartners.com

 

All applications and conversations are upheld with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

 

Why New Berlin, WI?

New Berlin, Wisconsin blends suburban comfort with convenient access to the greater Milwaukee area. Residents enjoy local parks, community events, family-friendly neighborhoods, and easy access to dining, shopping, and cultural attractions. With a welcoming community spirit and solid quality of life, New Berlin is an excellent place for both established veterinarians and new professionals to live, work, and thrive — all while being minutes from major metro amenities.

Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Job Description
Mission Pet Health is seeking a Regional Medical Director (RMD) to support our hospitals in Colorado, including the southern Denver metro area, Castle Rock, Colorado Springs, Eagle, Basalt, Glenwood Springs and Grand Junction.
This role is designed for a veterinarian who enjoys mentoring doctors, strengthening hospital teams, and helping practices grow while maintaining high medical quality.
As Regional Medical Director, you will serve as a clinical partner to our veterinarians and hospitals, supporting teams at the local level while working alongside a Regional Operations Director to align medical leadership with hospital success. The role blends mentorship, collaboration, and strategic leadership—ensuring doctors feel supported, hospitals are well-resourced, and patients receive excellent care.
What You'll Do
Support and Develop Veterinarians: Partner with DVMs across the region to support their professional growth through mentorship, coaching, and development planning. Conduct doctor reviews and help strengthen medical, surgical, and communication skills.
Strengthen Hospital Collaboration: Connect regularly with hospital teams to share best practices, support workflow improvements, and ensure doctors have the clinical resources they need to serve their communities.
Recruit and Develop Veterinary Talent: Help attract and onboard great veterinarians to the region. Support mentorship and integration of new doctors to ensure long-term success and engagement.
Partner for Hospital Success: Work closely with your Regional Operations Director to support hospital leaders in areas such as scheduling needs, equipment investments, and aligning medical initiatives with hospital growth.
Support Growth in Colorado: Help hospitals expand services, strengthen medical capabilities, and support thoughtful regional growth while maintaining high-quality medicine.
Build the Future Veterinary Workforce: Support externship and internship programs and represent Mission Pet Health within the veterinary community to help build our future talent pipeline.
What Success Looks Like in the First Year
First 90 Days: Build relationships with veterinarians and hospital teams while learning the strengths and opportunities of each hospital in the region.
3–6 Months: Establish regular doctor collaboration and mentorship, support hospital teams with clinical resources, and assist with recruiting and onboarding veterinarians.
6–12 Months: Strengthen collaboration across hospitals, support service expansion where appropriate, and help develop emerging medical leaders within the region.
Why This Role is Different
At Mission Pet Health, our Regional Medical Directors focus on supporting hospitals—not overseeing them from a distance.
We believe great veterinary medicine happens when doctors feel trusted, connected, and supported. Our RMDs focus on:
Mentorship over management
Local hospital partnership

Collaboration across veterinary teams
Balancing medical quality with sustainable hospital growth
This is an opportunity to help shape the veterinary culture of a region—supporting great hospitals and helping veterinarians build long-term, fulfilling careers.

What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree from an accredited university.
Current state licensure in good standing to practice.
Experience managing multi-site veterinary hospitals is required.
Hospital ownership or previous Lead Veterinarian experience is strongly preferred.
Exceptional interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills with the ability to present key data concisely.
Strong organizational skills and a keen attention to detail.

Job Description

Additional Hospital Information & Who We are Looking For: 

• Interest In: Small-animal general practice, preventative care, wellness exams, soft tissue surgery, orthopedic surgery, dental care, senior wellness, urgent care capabilities, physical rehabilitation, chiropractic care, acupuncture, internal medicine, end-of-life care, dermatology/allergy testing, diagnostics, and boarding support services.

 

• Open to New Graduates or Experienced Veterinarians: This practice welcomes both new and experienced doctors! New graduates will find a supportive team ready to help build clinical confidence, while experienced veterinarians can immediately contribute meaningfully to the patient caseload and practice growth.

 

• Supportive, Experienced Technical Staff: A dedicated team passionate about pets and veterinary medicine, focused on quality patient care, client communication, and technical support across diagnostics, anesthesia, dental and surgical procedures, and urgent care workflows.

 

• Layout Specifics: A comprehensive, well-equipped facility providing routine and advanced medical care options including surgery suites, treatment areas, rehabilitation space, boarding accommodations, and client-focused exam rooms — designed to support efficient workflows and excellent patient experiences.

 

• Collaborative Team Environment: Long-standing community presence since the 1940s with a team that values relationships with clients and pets, strong communication, and compassionate care for patients of all life stages.

 

• Patient Mix: Primarily dogs and cats, with robust preventive and urgent care cases paired with opportunities to participate in additional services (chiropractic, acupuncture, and allied disciplines) for veterinarians with those interests, making this environment ideal for doctors seeking a diverse small-animal practice.

 

Website: https://www.bemidjivethospital.com/

Location: 3610 Comfort Dr NW, Bemidji, MN 56601

Apply today for immediate consideration! If you have the drive, passion, and medical skills we’re looking for, we’d love to meet you.  For confidential consideration, please send your resume to Katie Shaffer at: katie.shaffer@mvetpartners.com

All applications and conversations are upheld with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

 

About Bemidji, MN

Located in northern Minnesota, Bemidji offers a high quality of life with the perfect blend of outdoor recreation, community feel, and everyday convenience. Surrounded by lakes, forests, and scenic trails, Bemidji is ideal for those who enjoy boating, fishing, hiking, biking, and all four seasons of outdoor adventure.

The city is home to a welcoming, close-knit community, a vibrant arts and music scene, local restaurants and breweries, and Bemidji State University, bringing energy and culture to the area. With a lower cost of living, minimal commute times, and excellent access to nature, Bemidji is an excellent place to build both a career and a fulfilling life outside of work.

Job Title: Regional Medical Director- Los Angeles and Las Vegas
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Mission Pet Health is seeking a Regional Medical Director (RMD) to support our hospitals in the Los Angeles and Las Vegas areas.
This role is designed for a veterinarian who enjoys mentoring doctors, strengthening hospital teams, and helping practices grow while maintaining high medical quality.
As Regional Medical Director, you will serve as a clinical partner to our veterinarians and hospitals, supporting teams at the local level while working alongside a Regional Operations Director to align medical leadership with hospital success. The role blends mentorship, collaboration, and strategic leadership—ensuring doctors feel supported, hospitals are well-resourced, and patients receive excellent care.
What You'll Do
Support and Develop Veterinarians: Partner with DVMs across the region to support their professional growth through mentorship, coaching, and development planning. Conduct doctor reviews and help strengthen medical, surgical, and communication skills.
Strengthen Hospital Collaboration: Connect regularly with hospital teams to share best practices, support workflow improvements, and ensure doctors have the clinical resources they need to serve their communities.
Recruit and Develop Veterinary Talent: Help attract and onboard great veterinarians to the region. Support mentorship and integration of new doctors to ensure long-term success and engagement.
Partner for Hospital Success: Work closely with your Regional Operations Director to support hospital leaders in areas such as scheduling needs, equipment investments, and aligning medical initiatives with hospital growth.
Support Growth in the LA/Vegas Market: Help hospitals expand services, strengthen medical capabilities, and support thoughtful regional growth while maintaining high-quality medicine.
Build the Future Veterinary Workforce: Support externship and internship programs and represent Mission Pet Health within the veterinary community to help build our future talent pipeline.
What Success Looks Like in the First Year
First 90 Days: Build relationships with veterinarians and hospital teams while learning the strengths and opportunities of each hospital in the region.
3–6 Months: Establish regular doctor collaboration and mentorship, support hospital teams with clinical resources, and assist with recruiting and onboarding veterinarians.
6–12 Months: Strengthen collaboration across hospitals, support service expansion where appropriate, and help develop emerging medical leaders within the region.
Why This Role is Different
At Mission Pet Health, our Regional Medical Directors focus on supporting hospitals—not overseeing them from a distance.
We believe great veterinary medicine happens when doctors feel trusted, connected, and supported. Our RMDs focus on:
Mentorship over management
Local hospital partnership

Collaboration across veterinary teams
Balancing medical quality with sustainable hospital growth
This is an opportunity to help shape the veterinary culture of a region—supporting great hospitals and helping veterinarians build long-term, fulfilling careers.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree from an accredited university.
Current state licensure in good standing to practice.
Experience managing multi-site veterinary hospitals is required.
Hospital ownership or previous Lead Veterinarian experience is strongly preferred.
Exceptional interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills with the ability to present key data concisely.
Strong organizational skills and a keen attention to detail.

Job Title: Lead Veterinarian Opportunity at Roseway Veterinary Hospital
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description
Your Impact as a Medical Lead Veterinarian

As the Medical Lead Veterinarian, you are the champion of medical excellence in your hospital. You will be responsible for mentoring and developing your associate veterinarians, partnering with your Practice Manager to build a healthy and engaged team, and driving the continuous improvement of patient care and client service. This is a key leadership role where you will set the standard for clinical quality and foster a culture of collaboration and growth.

Lead and Mentor Your DVM Team: You will manage all associate veterinarians, conducting regular performance discussions, coaching them on individual development goals (including production and PCT), and fostering a culture of teamwork and retention.
Role Model Medical Excellence: You will set the example for the highest quality of medicine, client service, and efficiency, while also practicing as an associate veterinarian and achieving your own production goals.
Drive Hospital Performance: In partnership with your Practice Manager, you will help develop the annual operating plan, manage hospital financial performance toward an EBITDA goal, and ensure compliance with all medical and DEA regulations.
Foster a Culture of Growth: You will lead monthly doctor meetings to discuss best practices, new services, and medical standards. You will also supervise externship and mentorship programs and champion a culture of recruitment.
Be an Ambassador: You will serve as an ambassador for your hospital and Mission Pet Health through community engagement at schools, universities, and local events.
What You'll Bring to the Team
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree and a valid state license in good standing.
A minimum of 2 years of practical clinical experience.
A servant leadership mentality with a commitment to our core values and the ability to build strong, collaborative relationships.
Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
An understanding of (or willingness to learn) the financial and operating management of a hospital.
Proficiency in intermediate computer skills (MS Office, Outlook, etc.).
Enthusiasm and a desire to be part of a progressive, growth-oriented culture.
Will Accept a New Grad?
No

Hospital Name: Livewell Animal Urgent Care of Bothell
Description:
=== PAGE CONTENT ===
Job Description

Livewell Animal Urgent Care of Bothell is a beautiful new hospital led by Founding DVM Dr. Steven Beil and Hospital Manager Candy Cooper. Together, they’ve built a hospital where high-quality medicine and a truly supportive culture go hand-in-hand.

 

We invite you to consider joining the team as our very first Associate DVM. This is an exceptional opportunity to be part of a hospital’s legacy from the ground up. You won’t just fill a role—you’ll help set the pace, shape the culture, and influence how our care is delivered for years to come.

 

Our urgent care model bridges the gap between general practice and emergency. We’re here for pets who can’t wait for a regular appointment, but don’t need full ER hospitalization.

 

That means interesting cases, steady variety, with more convenient hours

You’ll work alongside a leadership team deeply committed to collaboration, mentorship, and joy in our work. At Livewell, we believe a happy, cohesive team delivers the best medicine.

 

Why you’ll love this role:

Be part of the hospital’s story as our very first associate
Influence our culture, workflows, and client experience
Enjoy a varied caseload with a schedule that allows for better work/life balance
Practice with full medical autonomy and strong peer support
Join a leadership team that puts people first

Why Bothell?
Living and working in Bothell means enjoying a close-knit community feel while still having all the amenities of a central metro area within reach. It’s an area that supports a balanced lifestyle—one where you can pursue your career in veterinary medicine and still make the most of your time outside the hospital.

 

If you’re ready to make an impact, grow your skills, and help write Livewell Animal Urgent Care’s story, we’d love to meet you.

Job Description

Additional Hospital Information & Who We are Looking For: 

Interest In: Small-animal general practice, preventative care, wellness exams, surgery, dentistry, senior pet care, urgent care cases, end-of-life care, and compassionate client communication. Exposure to advanced diagnostics including imaging (CT/MRI through referral collaboration), chemotherapy support, and exotic pet care is a plus.
Open to New Graduates or Experienced Veterinarians: Open to both! New graduates are welcomed with tailored mentorship, while experienced veterinarians will have the autonomy to practice high-quality medicine and continue growing their clinical interests.
Supportive, Experienced Technical Staff: Dedicated and knowledgeable support team focused on patient care, client education, anesthesia support, laboratory diagnostics, dentistry, and smooth appointment flow — allowing doctors to focus on medicine while delivering an excellent client experience.
Layout Specifics: Well-equipped, AAHA-accredited facility featuring multiple exam rooms, a dedicated surgery suite, full dental equipment, in-house diagnostics, radiology, advanced monitoring, and a thoughtfully designed treatment area to promote efficiency and low-stress visits.
Collaborative Team Environment: Join a team that values compassion, communication, and community involvement. Doctors support one another, share knowledge, and work together to provide “peace of mind” care for both pets and their families.
Patient Mix: Primarily small-animal general practice seeing dogs and cats, with the added opportunity to work with a well-established exotic caseload for veterinarians who have a strong interest in exotics. While experience or interest in exotics is not required, the hospital offers exposure to a wide variety of exotic species for doctors who are excited to expand or utilize that skill set.
You do have an interest in exotics? We see small animals and a robust exotic caseload. Our team has expertise with birds, ferrets, rabbits, chinchillas, rats, mice, gerbils, guinea pigs, hamsters, raccoons, skunks, snakes, a variety of lizards (including chameleons, bearded dragons, and iguanas), turtles, tortoises, frogs, and hedgehogs. See more here (even deer!): https://www.denherdervet.com/service/exotic-pets/ 

Website: https://www.denherdervet.com/

Location: 4041 Hurst Dr, Waterloo, IA 50701

Apply today for immediate consideration! If you have the drive, passion, and medical skills we’re looking for, we’d love to meet you.  For confidential consideration, please send your resume to Katie Shaffer at: katie.shaffer@mvetpartners.com

All applications and conversations are upheld with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

Why Waterloo, IA?

Located in northeast Iowa, Waterloo offers the perfect balance of small-city charm and everyday convenience. The area features an affordable cost of living, short commutes, and a strong sense of community, making it an ideal place to put down roots. Outdoor enthusiasts will enjoy nearby parks, trails, and river access, while the city also offers local restaurants, breweries, arts and culture, and family-friendly activities. With easy access to larger metro areas like Cedar Falls and reasonable drives to Des Moines, Iowa City, and Minneapolis, Waterloo provides a comfortable lifestyle without the stress or expense of a major city.

We are a modern, upscale, patient-focused hospital offering refined client experience. At this time, we are looking for an experienced veterinarian with 2+ years of experience to join our high-performing team.

The ideal candidate is interested in: Small-animal general practice that practices fear free medicine. We provide the best of care for surgery, dentistry, preventative medicine, dermatology, internal med, vaccines, urgent care services, acupuncture, chiropractic care, as well as a focus on nutrition and behavior.

•
Supportive, Experienced Technical Staff: Highly skilled, long-tenured support staff with strong capabilities.
•
Layout Specifics: We are a small hospital, but new and built for the utilization of space!
•
Our Team: We are a practice with a wonderful culture!
What Your Day-to-Day Looks Like
•
Build Lasting Relationships: Connect with clients and their pets, becoming their trusted partner in a pet's lifelong health.
•
Practice Your Best Medicine: From diagnosis to treatment, you'll lead patient care with the freedom to uphold the highest standards.
•
Educate and Empower: Clearly communicate findings and treatment plans, empowering clients to make informed decisions.
•
Collaborate and Grow: Work alongside a supportive paraprofessional team, contributing to a positive hospital culture.
Who We're Looking For
•
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree from an accredited university.
•
A valid state license and in good standing to practice in Massachusetts.
•
A collaborative spirit and a dedication to practicing the highest standard of medicine.
•
Excellent communication skills with a positive, friendly, and professional attitude.

Apply today for immediate consideration! If you have the drive, passion, and medical skills we're looking for, we'd love to meet you.

For confidential consideration, please send your resume to Vanessa Sanchez at Vanessa.sanchez@missionpethealth.com. All applications and conversations are upheld with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

Location: 93 Hyde Park Ave Boston, MA 02130
Website: metrovetclinic.com
